lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- He Said, She Said -- Reported Speech
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Mark Kazuyoshi Seki Omagari | Aula 6 -- He Said, She Said

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome to the nine o'clock news. Tonight, our top headline: the city has announced a brand new sports centre for young people. A spokesperson for the mayor revealed that building work would begin next month and that the centre would open the following year. According to local witnesses, the news was a complete surprise. One neighbour claimed that nobody in the area had heard anything about the plan before today. However, the council denied that the project had been kept secret, and a spokesperson said that all the documents had been public for weeks. Some people online began to gossip and exaggerate, saying the centre would cost a billion dollars, but officials confirmed that this rumour was simply not true. We will bring you more on this story as it develops. That is all for tonight. Good night.

Hey everyone, welcome back to the School Sports Podcast. So, big news this week about the football final. After the match, the coach told reporters that the team had played the best game of the season. She said that the players had trained really hard and that she was proud of every single one of them. The captain revealed that he had been nervous before the game, but he claimed that the crowd had given him the energy to keep going. Now, there is a rumour going around that two players might leave next year, but the club has denied it. A spokesperson announced that nobody was leaving and asked fans not to exaggerate or spread gossip. According to the official report, the team will stay together for at least one more season. That is the news for today. See you next week.

extra_audio.text: First, something happens at school and Mark sees it. Then a reporter asks Mark what people said. Mark tells the reporter that the coach announced the news. Next, Mark reports that the captain claimed the team would win. Finally, the reporter writes the headline and broadcasts the story.
